checklists.title: Activate San Ramon home services
steps: Confirm the service address is inside San Ramon and identify the responsible provider for each service.
Record whether water and wastewater are handled by the applicable district or another provider.
Open electricity and gas service for the possession date.
Arrange garbage and recycling service and learn cart, collection and holiday rules.
Arrange internet or communications service if needed.
Save account numbers, start dates, deposits, confirmations and outage contacts.

checklists.title: Enroll in San Ramon emergency alerts
steps: Enroll each capable adult using the current City-supported alert channel.
Use the San Ramon ZIP-code text enrollment option when applicable.
Save the SRPD live-alert page and local emergency radio information.
Enable alerts on phones and verify they are not silenced by device settings.
Add an out-of-area emergency contact and review enrollment twice a year.

checklists.title: Create San Ramon resident accounts
steps: List city services the household expects to use.
Create the recreation account and accurately record household members and residency information.
Create a Citizen Request Management account using a unique password.
Save official portal addresses in a password manager rather than relying on search advertisements.
Record support contacts and test account recovery.

checklists.title: Assess household emergency needs
steps: List every household member, pet and regular visitor who may need help.
Record medications, medical devices, mobility, communication, dietary and power needs.
Identify likely local hazards and safe shelter or evacuation options.
Assign roles that do not depend on one person being present.
Document insurance, property, identity and emergency-contact information securely.

checklists.title: Create a family emergency plan
steps: Select nearby and out-of-neighborhood meeting places.
Choose an out-of-area contact and define text, call and check-in methods.
Map at least two evacuation routes and a shelter-in-place location.
Plan for school, work, commuting, pets and separated household members.
Print a compact copy and store offline copies on household phones.

checklists.title: Build emergency supply kits
steps: Build a stay-at-home kit with water, food, light, radio, sanitation and first aid.
Build portable go kits for household members and pets.
Add medications, copies of essential records, cash, chargers and specialty supplies.
Label storage dates and rotate water, food, batteries and medications.
Start with at least 72 hours and expand toward seven days where practical.

checklists.title: Review San Ramon local hazards
steps: Review earthquake shaking risk and secure heavy furniture and utilities.
Review wildfire evacuation routes and smoke preparation.
Check address-specific creek or flood exposure.
Consider hazardous-material transportation incidents near major routes.
Record the correct shelter, evacuation and alert sources for each hazard.

checklists.title: Select the correct San Ramon permit type
steps: Describe the project scope, address, structure, systems and site work.
Check whether building, planning, engineering, fire or right-of-way approval may apply.
Contact the Permit Center before submission when the category is uncertain.
Select the exact portal permit type because a submitted type may not be changeable.
Record required drawings, calculations, owner authorizations and supporting permits.

checklists.title: Submit a San Ramon permit
steps: Create or verify the official permit-portal account.
Upload complete, legible and consistently named plans and documents.
Confirm contractor and property-owner information.
Review estimated fees and submit the correct application.
Save the permit number, receipt, uploaded package and all correction notices.

checklists.title: Manage San Ramon permit inspections
steps: Read the approved plans and inspection sequence before work begins.
Keep permit documents available at the site.
Request each inspection only after the relevant work is ready and accessible.
Record results and correct deficiencies without concealing work prematurely.
Obtain final approval and retain the closed permit with property records.

checklists.title: Triage a public service issue
steps: Stop and call emergency services when life, fire, active crime or immediate danger is involved.
Use the designated urgent non-emergency channel when the issue cannot wait for business hours.
Use the routine service-request channel for maintenance or administrative issues.
Do not enter traffic, private property or unsafe areas to gather evidence.

checklists.title: Submit a San Ramon CRM request
steps: Choose the closest official topic and confirm it is not an emergency.
Provide the precise address, intersection or mapped location.
Describe what happened, when it began and the ongoing impact.
Attach only safe, relevant photos without unnecessary personal information.
Save the tracking number and monitor the recorded response.

checklists.title: Check San Ramon recreation registration timing
steps: Open the current quarterly or camp recreation guide.
Record the resident and non-resident opening dates and time.
Confirm space, waitlist and age-cutoff rules for the selected program.
Set a reminder before registration opens and prepare account and payment details.

checklists.title: Verify San Ramon recreation eligibility
steps: Confirm whether the address is within San Ramon city limits.
Verify that household members are recorded under the correct parent or spouse relationship.
Confirm age, skill, prerequisite and residency documentation requirements.
Check resident versus non-resident pricing before checkout.

checklists.title: Review San Ramon recreation policies
steps: Review payment methods and current transaction fees.
Review withdrawal, refund, credit and transfer deadlines.
Request inclusion or special-needs support early when required.
Review conduct, equipment, supervision and pickup rules.
Save the receipt, policy version and program contact.
